title: Diosma obtusa
taxonomy: Diosma obtusa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This plant is native to the Western Cape province in South Africa. Diosma obtusa is a member of the genus Diosma, which contains about 60 species of shrubs. The genus Diosma is further classified within the tribe Rutoideae, which includes several species of flowering plants. The classification of Diosma obtusa reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Rutaceae family.

intro: Diosma obtusa, also known as the Cape buchu or mountain buchu, is a low-growing, woody shrub native to the mountains of South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to aid in digestion, relieve flatulence, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it beneficial for the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues. Diosma obtusa contains bioactive constituents such as terpenoids, flavonoids, and essential oils, particularly borneol, bornyl acetate, and diosmin, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are used to treat digestive issues, promote oral health, and provide relief from respiratory problems.
